changes: OpenClaw shipped v2026.4.15-beta.2 at 2026-04-16 19:30 UTC and the stable v2026.4.15 at 2026-04-16 21:50 UTC, completing another fast beta-to-stable cycle.
Hermes Agent released v2026.4.16 at 2026-04-16 19:53 UTC while repo momentum climbed to roughly 95k stars by the 2026-04-17 scan.
ZeroClaw posted a fresh prerelease, v0.7.0-beta.1030, at 2026-04-17 04:56 UTC, keeping the ultra-light Rust lane active.
SwarmClaw published v1.5.59, v1.5.60, and v1.5.61 between 2026-04-16 23:53 UTC and 2026-04-17 00:07 UTC, extending its rapid control-plane cadence.
PicoClaw posted another nightly build at 2026-04-17 01:02 UTC, keeping the edge-device lane warm without a new stable release.
whyItMatters: OpenClaw is still setting the category tempo by converting previews into stable cuts quickly, not just shipping noisy betas.
Hermes continues to strengthen the learning-loop benchmark, increasing pressure on the rest of the ecosystem to differentiate beyond basic tool orchestration.
ZeroClaw’s prerelease cadence reinforces that the lightweight Rust lane is still an active competitive branch.
SwarmClaw remains the clearest sign that a control-plane and operator layer is forming around OpenClaw-like runtimes.
PicoClaw still validates the cheap-hardware thesis, though today’s signal was maintenance cadence rather than a strategic jump.
